visits.note: Patient presents with symptoms of fatigue, polyuria, and polydipsia for 2 months. Physical examination reveals normal vitals, no palpable masses, and normal neurological examination. Diagnoses include Type 2 Diabetes and Hypertension. Plan includes initiation of metformin and lisinopril.

Patient returns with worsening symptoms of fatigue and increased urination. Physical examination reveals normal vitals, no palpable masses, and normal neurological examination. BP is now 150/90. Plan includes adjustment of metformin to 1000mg BID and initiation of atorvastatin for Hyperlipidemia.

Patient returns with worsening symptoms of dyspnea and fatigue. Physical examination reveals normal vitals, but with bilateral rales. BP is now 160/95. eGFR has decreased to 55. Plan includes initiation of spironolactone for CKD and adjustment of lisinopril to 20mg OD.

Patient returns with worsening symptoms of orthopnea and fatigue. Physical examination reveals normal vitals, but with bilateral rales. BP is now 165/100. eGFR has decreased to 45. Plan includes adjustment of metformin to 500mg BID and initiation of enalapril for CHF.
